---
format: html
title: KNN Regression Notes
---

# KNN Regression

## 1. Problem setup

Given training data $$
\{(x_i,y_i)\}_{i=1}^n,\quad x_i\in\mathbb R^p,\; y_i\in\mathbb R,
$$

we estimate $$
f(x)=\mathbb E[Y\mid X=x].
$$

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2. Model form

$$
\hat f(x_0)
=
\frac{1}{K}\sum_{i\in N_K(x_0)} y_i
$$

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 3. Geometry view

-   local neighborhood
-   adaptive region size

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 4. Weight form

$$
\hat f(x_0)=\sum_{i=1}^n w_i(x_0)y_i
$$

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 5. Distance

$$
d(x,x')=\sqrt{\sum_{j=1}^p (x_j-x'_j)^2}
$$

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 6. Bias--variance

-   small $K$: low bias, high variance\
-   large $K$: high bias, low variance

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 7. Curse of dimensionality

-   high $p$ → sparse neighbors\
-   performance degrades

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 8. Comparison

-   linear model: global\
-   KNN: local

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 9. Key takeaway

KNN = adaptive local averaging
